# Workspace Lock Policy

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/workspacenativeconfigdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to native config manifest](../native-config-manifest.md)

This document owns lock usage policy in domain language. The concrete `flock` or DB lease primitive is owned by C9 Locking.

| Lock | Scope | Hold time | Use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `repo_cache_update.lock` | `cache/repos/{repo_hash}` | Short; fetch/prune only | Cache update |
| `task_workdir.lock` | `workspaces/.../runs/{run_id}/` | For the run | Prevent adapter, RunController, and validation from mutating the same workdir concurrently |
| `archive_pipeline.lock` | Archive phase | Archive only | `ArchiveWorkspace` |

## Forbidden

- Do not wrap an entire agent run in `repo_lock`; same-repo tasks must not become globally serialized.
- Do not use one task's `task_workdir.lock` to protect another task's workdir; isolation is directory separation plus protected path and sandbox policy.

## Distributed Environments

When multiple daemons share one host/cache, `repo_cache_update.lock` is meaningful. Cross-host cache sharing is outside this SSOT and normally uses host-local caches.
